$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `llm_logs` repo — the SECOND sibling-DB
partition of Phase 2 (after the mount-index family) and the WIDEST repo to
date (18 columns, FIVE nested JSON-object columns). One committed file drives
BOTH sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-llm-logs-fixture.ts + llm-logs-tier2.ts) and
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness llm_logs tier-2 test).
SIBLING DB: in v4 this table lives in quilltap-llm-logs.db (NOT the main DB).
The builder/oracle point SQLITE_LLM_LOGS_PATH at the fixture and read it back
through getRawLLMLogsDatabase(); the Rust Writer simply opens that file by
path. v4's LLMLogsRepository.getCollection() lazily creates the table on first
access (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS), so calling repo.create in the builder
stands the table up — no explicit ensureCollection.
MARSHALING SURFACE banked here: the five nested JSON-object columns (request,
response, usage, cacheUsage, requestHashes — typed-struct, schema field order,
compact JSON.stringify), the open-JSON rawProviderUsage column (CONSTRAINED to
null / {} / single-key only — serde_json sorts keys, seam #5), a nullable REAL
durationMs column (integer-valued collapses to a JSON int in the dump), the
type enum TEXT column, and four nullable UUID-as-TEXT columns. Nested numbers:
integer-valued fields render as integers (i64; messageCount/contentLength/
toolCount/maxTokens/promptTokens/...), temperature stays fractional or omitted,
and fullMessages/toolCalls/contentPreview are OMITTED everywhere.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one every other
tier-2 fixture uses. It is NOT the real Friday pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.

seed.$comment: Rich row: all linkage UUIDs set, usage + cacheUsage + requestHashes present, rawProviderUsage single-key, durationMs integer, 2 messages, temperature fractional.
Sparse row: nullable UUIDs null, usage/cacheUsage/requestHashes null, rawProviderUsage null, durationMs null, 1 message, temperature omitted.

messages.content: Summarize this chat in a title.

seed.$comment: Throwaway row destined for deletion (exercises delete without losing any banked shape from the surviving rows).

ops.comment: New SURVIVING log: rawProviderUsage as EMPTY object {}, chatId set but the rest of the linkage null, cacheUsage with one of the two optional fields omitted, durationMs integer. Banks {} in the final state. Type changed to ANSWER_CONFIRMATION to bank v4's new LLMLogType enum member (a plain TEXT column on the port side, no marshaling change).
Swap the response object AND set usage on the sparse seed row 2. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned. Also sets durationMs (was null) to an integer.
Remove the throwaway IMAGE_GENERATION seed row.
